content: The orthographic and lexical teaching of "reflexion" as an accepted variant spelling of reflection in high school English education hints at a natural parallel: LLM self-reflection mechanisms can benefit from retaining dual "variant representations" of problem-solving trajectories, just as language retains two correct spellings of the same core concept. This allows LLMs to pull from inconsistent, equally valid reasoning paths to improve self-reflection quality, rather than overwriting old trajectories with a single "correct" approach.
